notes.title: Open Court packets matter
notes.note: Open Court packets are valuable because they hit skills and topics students might not otherwise practice.

notes.title: Preteach vocabulary
notes.connectedTo: Lesson 1: The Mice Who Lived in a Shoe
notes.note: Teach vocabulary before reading so students are not stopped by unfamiliar words.

notes.title: Merchant needs extra support
notes.note: Spend extra time on merchant because many students do not use that word in daily speech.

notes.title: Lesson 6 fluency
notes.note: Use the fluency checklist before reading A Trip. Have students listen for rate, accuracy, expression, and punctuation.

notes.title: Routines save instruction
notes.note: Teach fewer routines deeply rather than many routines quickly.

notes.title: Line up practice matters
notes.note: If you spend ten minutes teaching line-up expectations, you save hours of transition time later.

notes.title: Dismissal needs extra time
notes.note: Start dismissal earlier than you think during the first week. Calm dismissal is taught.

notes.title: Habitats in a Shoebox
notes.note: This is a yearly success. Students love it and admin praises the implementation.

notes.title: Volcano assignment
notes.note: The volcano assignment works well because it covers topics students might not otherwise get from the main lesson.

notes.title: Preview videos
notes.note: Preview videos before showing them to students.

notes.title: iDay planning
notes.note: iDays need a different planning workflow with MobyMax and Beanstack.

notes.title: Connect Social Studies to Open Court
notes.note: Social Studies lessons should connect to Open Court stories whenever possible.

notes.title: Model before expecting
notes.note: Do not assume students know routines. Model it, practice it, praise it, and repeat.

notes.title: Partner talk setup
notes.note: Assign Partner A and Partner B clearly or students lose time deciding.
